title: crocus lageniflorus subvar. lacteus
intro: Crocus lageniflorus subvar. lacteus, also known as the white-flowered crocus or white lily-flowered crocus, is a subspecies of the crocus lageniflorus plant, a species of flowering plant in the iris family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Crocus lageniflorus subvar. lacteus include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or inflorescence, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its medicinal properties.
